diff_quality.notes: Clean, idiomatic Django. Integration isolated in api/cometchat.py using only stdlib urllib (no new deps), env-driven, inert when unconfigured so build/CI stay green. Best-effort safe() wrapper prevents chat side-effects from breaking core API. REST fullAccess key kept server-side; clients receive only App ID + Region + minted auth token. Correct v3 REST base URL and endpoint shapes. verify.sh extended to assert route resolution + inert behavior.

queries.query: REST API create user and create auth token server side
REST API create group add members owner

coverage_gaps: Create-user REST page does not document the 'already exists' error code (agent had to guess ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS from the Groups error table) — no idempotent re-provisioning / conflict-handling guidance for POST /users.

No backend/server-side auth-token-minting recipe surfaced for a Python/Django framework specifically; agent stitched it together from individual REST endpoint pages (worked, but no end-to-end backend guide).

Docs do not explain how a non-participant (parent/admin) actually gains read-only access to another user's 1:1 or group conversations — setting a `role` string on a CometChat user does not by itself enforce or grant read access.
staleness_conflicts: UID character constraints conflict across docs: /rest-api/users/create says 'Alphanumeric with dashes only' while SDK Key Concepts pages say 'alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen.' Agent had to pick the stricter rule to be safe.

blockers.issue: The RBAC scope 'parent reads own child's threads' and 'admin audits' is not actually implemented — the backend only returns can_send/can_call=false flags plus conversation identifiers, and relies on the client honoring them. Parents/admins are never added to the 1:1/group conversations and no read proxy exists, so there is no real mechanism by which they can read the threads. The read-only audit half of the RBAC requirement is effectively unimplemented.

The idempotent 'already exists' handling depends on error-code strings the agent could not confirm from docs (ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS, ERR_ALREADY_JOINED). If the real codes differ, repeat provisioning/enroll will raise CometChatError instead of being treated as success (fails loudly rather than silently, but breaks the intended idempotency).

E-ROLES — CometChat user provisioning fails on a fresh app: the integrated backend assigns role: student/tutor/parent/admin on POST /users, but custom roles must be CREATED (POST /roles) before any user can reference them — on a virgin CometChat app every user-provision call fails 'Failed to validate the data sent with the request' and the entire chat bootstrap is dead. Neither the skill nor docs mention role pre-creation; the integration agent wired RBAC roles it had no way to provision.

E-GROUP-NOPROV — group sessions have NO CometChat group, so ALL group chat + group voice/video calling is dead (half the calling-spec matrix). Two layers: (1) the seed (api/management/commands/seed.py:245) creates group Sessions directly via Session.objects.create(kind='group',...) and NEVER calls cometchat.ensure_group — provisioning only runs in the API create/enroll views, which the seed bypasses; (2) SessionChatView returns the descriptor with group_guid='session-<id>' and can_call=true WITHOUT verifying the CometChat group exists. Verified: GET /v3/groups/session-1 -> 'group with guid session-1 does not exist', 0 members. Opening a seeded masterclass room loads a non-existent group -> group chat/call fails. All ensure_group/add_group_member calls are wrapped in cometchat.safe() (errors swallowed), so even API-created groups fail silently.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: On the /rest-api/users/create doc page, add the conflict/'already exists' error code and an explicit idempotent create-or-update (POST → PUT on conflict) pattern, matching what the Groups error table already documents.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Idempotent provisioning relies on an unconfirmed ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS code (blocker: unconfirmed already-exists codes).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Reconcile the UID character-constraint wording between /rest-api/users/create ('alphanumeric with dashes only') and the SDK Key Concepts pages ('alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen') so a single canonical rule is documented.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Contradictory UID constraints (staleness_conflict) forced the agent to guess the stricter rule.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add a docs-mcp guide on observer / read-only access patterns (e.g. how a parent or admin actually reads another user's conversations — group membership with a restricted scope, or a server-side history proxy via REST), and clarify that a `role` string on a CometChat user does not itself enforce messaging or grant read permissions.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Parent/admin read-only audit RBAC was hand-waved and left unimplemented (blocker: RBAC read scope not implemented).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: The backend skill must create the app's roles (idempotent POST /roles) before assigning them — or document that role assignment requires prior role creation; every RBAC-enabled use case hits this on a fresh app
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: E-ROLES — CometChat user provisioning fails on a fresh app: the integrated backend assigns role: student/tutor/parent/admin on POST /users, but custom roles must be CREATED (POST /roles) before any user can reference them — on a virgin CometChat app every user-provision call fails 'Failed to validate the data sent with the request' and the entire chat bootstrap is dead. Neither the skill nor docs mention role pre-creation; the integration agent wired RBAC roles it had no way to provision.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Lazily ensure the CometChat group exists when the chat descriptor is requested (idempotent ensure_group + add current member in SessionChatView), so groups work regardless of how the session was created (seed, API, migration). And do not swallow provisioning errors silently — surface them so a broken group is visible
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: E-GROUP-NOPROV — group sessions have NO CometChat group, so ALL group chat + group voice/video calling is dead (half the calling-spec matrix). Two layers: (1) the seed (api/management/commands/seed.py:245) creates group Sessions directly via Session.objects.create(kind='group',...) and NEVER calls cometchat.ensure_group — provisioning only runs in the API create/enroll views, which the seed bypasses; (2) SessionChatView returns the descriptor with group_guid='session-<id>' and can_call=true WITHOUT verifying the CometChat group exists. Verified: GET /v3/groups/session-1 -> 'group with guid session-1 does not exist', 0 members. Opening a seeded masterclass room loads a non-existent group -> group chat/call fails. All ensure_group/add_group_member calls are wrapped in cometchat.safe() (errors swallowed), so even API-created groups fail silently.

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY

- **CometChat skill variant(s) relied on:** The `cometchat` dispatcher skill loaded but is oriented to frontend UI Kits (React/Angular/native), not a Python/Django backend — none of its per-framework code paths apply. For a backend service the authoritative source was **docs-mcp REST API** pages, which I used directly: `/rest-api/authentication`, `/rest-api/users/create`, `/rest-api/auth-tokens/create`, `/rest-api/groups/create`, `/rest-api/group-members`. These gave exact endpoints, headers (`apikey`), payloads, and error codes.
- **Where skills/docs were wrong/missing/contradictory:** (1) The dispatcher has no backend/server cohort — it assumes a client framework; I treated the REST docs as ground truth instead. (2) **UID character constraint conflicts across docs:** `/rest-api/users/create` says "Alphanumeric with dashes only," while the SDK "Key Concepts" pages say "alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen." I chose hyphens (`user-<id>`, `session-<id>`) to satisfy the stricter rule. (3) Docs describe the create/mint sequence but not idempotent re-provisioning behavior/error codes explicitly — I inferred `ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS`/`ERR_GUID_ALREADY_EXISTS` handling from the Groups error table and general patterns and coded defensive fallbacks (POST→PUT update on conflict; treat already-exists as success).
- **Candidate hallucinations (API surfaces I couldn't fully confirm from docs):** the exact error-code strings `ERR_UID_ALREADY_EXISTS` and `ERR_ALREADY_JOINED` — the Groups/Group-Members error tables confirm `ERR_GUID_ALREADY_EXISTS`, `ERR_NOT_A_MEMBER`, `ERR_UID_NOT_FOUND`, `ERR_ALREADY_JOINED`, but the user "already exists" code was not shown on the create-user page. My code degrades safely if the actual code differs (it just surfaces a `CometChatError` rather than silently mis-handling), so a wrong guess here fails loudly, not silently.
- **Web-search / guessing:** None. I stayed entirely within docs-mcp + the skill. No web searches.
- **Scope completed:** user↔CometChat mapping with roles; server-side auth-token minting (REST key server-only, never sent to clients); group-session→CometChat group with tutor-owner + enrollment membership sync; RBAC scoping (student/tutor converse; parent + admin read-only observers) surfaced via `/sessions/:id/chat`; secrets in env + gitignored; `docker build` gate green.
- **Left incomplete / out of scope (backend):** Actual message/history retrieval for parent/admin audit is not proxied through the backend — the design hands clients the conversation identifiers + read-only flags and relies on CometChat's own RBAC/SDK for enforcement (the `role` is set on each CometChat user for this). Calling is covered only at the identity/permission layer (`can_call`) since WebRTC/call UI is entirely client-side. No automated CometChat call is made in CI (would require live credentials); the token path is exercised via mocked transport instead.
